1→(* Pure OCaml game logic for switch/trigger functions.
     2→   Ported from main_d2/switch.cpp *)
     3→
     4→(* Wall flag constants *)
     5→let wall_door_locked = 8
     6→let key_none = 1
     7→
     8→(* -- Algebraic effects -------------------------------------------------- *)
     9→
    10→type _ Effect.t +=
    11→  | Fetch_trigger_links : int -> int array Effect.t
    12→      (* trigger_num -> [num_links, wallnum0, wallnum1, ...] *)
    13→  | Lock_wall_door : int -> unit Effect.t
    14→      (* wall_num -> sets WALL_DOOR_LOCKED flag *)
    15→  | Unlock_wall_door : int -> unit Effect.t
    16→      (* wall_num -> clears WALL_DOOR_LOCKED, sets keys=KEY_NONE *)
    17→  | Get_num_triggers : unit -> int Effect.t
    18→  | Get_num_walls : unit -> int Effect.t
    19→  | Set_wall_flag_wall_switch : int -> unit Effect.t
    20→  | Fetch_trigger_seg_sides : int -> int array Effect.t
    21→      (* trigger_num -> [num_links, seg0, side0, seg1, side1, ...] *)
    22→
    23→(* do_lock_doors: Locks all doors linked to the switch.
    24→   Ported from main_d2/switch.cpp *)
    25→let do_lock_doors ~trigger_num =
    26→  if trigger_num <> -1 then begin
    27→    let links = Effect.perform (Fetch_trigger_links trigger_num) in
    28→    let num_links = links.(0) in
    29→    for i = 0 to num_links - 1 do
    30→      let wall_num = links.(1 + i) in
    31→      Effect.perform (Lock_wall_door wall_num)
    32→    done
    33→  end
    34→
    35→(* do_unlock_doors: Unlocks all doors linked to the switch.
    36→   Ported from main_d2/switch.cpp *)
    37→let do_unlock_doors ~trigger_num =
    38→  if trigger_num <> -1 then begin
    39→    let links = Effect.perform (Fetch_trigger_links trigger_num) in
    40→    let num_links = links.(0) in
    41→    for i = 0 to num_links - 1 do
    42→      let wall_num = links.(1 + i) in
    43→      Effect.perform (Unlock_wall_door wall_num)
    44→    done
    45→  end
    46→
    47→(* door_is_wall_switched: Return trigger number if door is controlled by
    48→   a wall switch, else return -1.
    49→   Ported from main_d2/switch.cpp *)
    50→let door_is_wall_switched ~wall_num =
    51→  let num_triggers = Effect.perform (Get_num_triggers ()) in
    52→  let result = ref (-1) in
    53→  let found = ref false in
    54→  let t = ref 0 in
    55→  while !t < num_triggers && not !found do
    56→    let links = Effect.perform (Fetch_trigger_links !t) in
    57→    let num_links = links.(0) in
    58→    let i = ref 0 in
    59→    while !i < num_links && not !found do
    60→      if links.(1 + !i) = wall_num then begin
    61→        result := !t;
    62→        found := true
    63→      end;
    64→      incr i
    65→    done;
    66→    incr t
    67→  done;
    68→  !result
    69→
    70→(* flag_wall_switched_doors: Mark all wall-switched doors with WALL_WALL_SWITCH flag.
    71→   Ported from main_d2/switch.cpp *)
    72→let flag_wall_switched_doors () =
    73→  let num_walls = Effect.perform (Get_num_walls ()) in
    74→  for i = 0 to num_walls - 1 do
    75→    if door_is_wall_switched ~wall_num:i <> 0 then
    76→      Effect.perform (Set_wall_flag_wall_switch i)
    77→  done
    78→
    79→(* do_il_on: Turn on illusion walls for all links of a trigger.
    80→   Ported from switch.cpp (identical in D1 and D2). *)
    81→let do_il_on ~trigger_num =
    82→  if trigger_num <> -1 then begin
    83→    let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_trigger_seg_sides trigger_num) in
    84→    let num_links = data.(0) in
    85→    for i = 0 to num_links - 1 do
    86→      let segnum = data.(1 + i * 2) in
    87→      let side = data.(1 + i * 2 + 1) in
    88→      Ox_wall.wall_illusion_on ~segnum ~side
    89→    done
    90→  end
    91→

1→(* Bridge adapter for switch/trigger functions.
     2→   Uses algebraic effects: OCaml performs effects, C handler executes them. *)
     3→
     4→(* Effect externals *)
     5→external effect_fetch_trigger_links
     6→  :  int
     7→  -> int array
     8→  = "cd_ox_effect_sw_fetch_trigger_links"
     9→
    10→external effect_lock_wall_door
    11→  :  int
    12→  -> unit
    13→  = "cd_ox_effect_sw_lock_wall_door"
    14→
    15→external effect_unlock_wall_door
    16→  :  int
    17→  -> unit
    18→  = "cd_ox_effect_sw_unlock_wall_door"
    19→
    20→external effect_get_num_triggers
    21→  :  unit
    22→  -> int
    23→  = "cd_ox_effect_sw_get_num_triggers"
    24→
    25→external effect_get_num_walls
    26→  :  unit
    27→  -> int
    28→  = "cd_ox_effect_sw_get_num_walls"
    29→
    30→external effect_set_wall_flag_wall_switch
    31→  :  int
    32→  -> unit
    33→  = "cd_ox_effect_sw_set_wall_flag_wall_switch"
    34→
    35→external effect_fetch_trigger_seg_sides
    36→  :  int
    37→  -> int array
    38→  = "cd_ox_effect_sw_fetch_trigger_seg_sides"
    39→
    40→(* Effect handler *)
    41→let effc (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) : ((a, 'b) Effect.Deep.continuation -> 'b) option =
    42→  match eff with
    43→  | Ox_switch.Fetch_trigger_links trigger_num ->
    44→    Some (fun k ->
    45→      let data = effect_fetch_trigger_links trigger_num in
    46→      Effect.Deep.continue k data)
    47→  | Ox_switch.Lock_wall_door wall_num ->
    48→    Some (fun k ->
    49→      effect_lock_wall_door wall_num;
    50→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
    51→  | Ox_switch.Unlock_wall_door wall_num ->
    52→    Some (fun k ->
    53→      effect_unlock_wall_door wall_num;
    54→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
    55→  | Ox_switch.Get_num_triggers () ->
    56→    Some (fun k ->
    57→      let n = effect_get_num_triggers () in
    58→      Effect.Deep.continue k n)
    59→  | Ox_switch.Get_num_walls () ->
    60→    Some (fun k ->
    61→      let n = effect_get_num_walls () in
    62→      Effect.Deep.continue k n)
    63→  | Ox_switch.Set_wall_flag_wall_switch wall_num ->
    64→    Some (fun k ->
    65→      effect_set_wall_flag_wall_switch wall_num;
    66→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
    67→  | Ox_switch.Fetch_trigger_seg_sides trigger_num ->
    68→    Some (fun k ->
    69→      let data = effect_fetch_trigger_seg_sides trigger_num in
    70→      Effect.Deep.continue k data)
    71→  | _ -> None
    72→;;
    73→
    74→let handler =
    75→  { Effect.Deep.retc = (fun x -> x)
    76→  ; exnc = (fun e ->
    77→      Printf.eprintf "[OX] switch exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string e);
    78→      Out_channel.flush stderr;
    79→      -1)
    80→  ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> effc eff)
    81→  }
    82→
    83→(* Bridge entry points called from C via caml_callback *)
    84→let cd_do_lock_doors trigger_num =
    85→  Effect.Deep.match_with
    86→    (fun () -> Ox_switch.do_lock_doors ~trigger_num)
    87→    ()
    88→    { retc = (fun () -> ())
    89→    ; exnc = (fun e ->
    90→        Printf.eprintf "[OX] do_lock_doors exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string e);
    91→        Out_channel.flush stderr)
    92→    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> effc eff)
    93→    }
    94→;;
    95→
    96→let cd_do_unlock_doors trigger_num =
    97→  Effect.Deep.match_with
    98→    (fun () -> Ox_switch.do_unlock_doors ~trigger_num)
    99→    ()
   100→    { retc = (fun () -> ())
   101→    ; exnc = (fun e ->
   102→        Printf.eprintf "[OX] do_unlock_doors exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string e);
   103→        Out_channel.flush stderr)
   104→    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> effc eff)
   105→    }
   106→;;
   107→
   108→let cd_door_is_wall_switched wall_num =
   109→  Effect.Deep.match_with
   110→    (fun () -> Ox_switch.door_is_wall_switched ~wall_num)
   111→    ()
   112→    handler
   113→;;
   114→
   115→let cd_flag_wall_switched_doors () =
   116→  Effect.Deep.match_with
   117→    (fun () -> Ox_switch.flag_wall_switched_doors ())
   118→    ()
   119→    { retc = (fun () -> ())
   120→    ; exnc = (fun e ->
   121→        Printf.eprintf "[OX] flag_wall_switched_doors exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string e);
   122→        Out_channel.flush stderr)
   123→    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> effc eff)
   124→    }
   125→;;
   126→
   127→let combined_effc (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) : ((a, 'b) Effect.Deep.continuation -> 'b) option =
   128→  match effc eff with
   129→  | Some _ as s -> s
   130→  | None -> Wall.effc eff
   131→;;
   132→
   133→let cd_do_il_on trigger_num =
   134→  Effect.Deep.match_with
   135→    (fun () -> Ox_switch.do_il_on ~trigger_num)
   136→    ()
   137→    { retc = (fun () -> ())
   138→    ; exnc = (fun e ->
   139→        Printf.eprintf "[OX] do_il_on exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string e);
   140→        Out_channel.flush stderr)
   141→    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> combined_effc eff)
   142→    }
   143→;;
   144→
   145→let register_callbacks () =
   146→  Callback.register "cd_do_lock_doors" cd_do_lock_doors;
   147→  Callback.register "cd_do_unlock_doors" cd_do_unlock_doors;
   148→  Callback.register "cd_door_is_wall_switched" cd_door_is_wall_switched;
   149→  Callback.register "cd_flag_wall_switched_doors" cd_flag_wall_switched_doors;
   150→  Callback.register "cd_do_il_on" cd_do_il_on
   151→;;
   152→

1→(* Pure OCaml game logic for wall functions.
     2→   Ported from main_d1/wall.cpp and main_d2/wall.cpp *)
     3→
     4→(* Constants *)
     5→let max_walls_d1 = 175
     6→let max_walls_d2 = 254
     7→let wall_normal = 0
     8→
     9→(* -- Algebraic effects -------------------------------------------------- *)
    10→
    11→type _ Effect.t +=
    12→  | Fetch_reset_walls_info : unit -> int array Effect.t
    13→  | Write_reset_walls : int array -> unit Effect.t
    14→  | Fetch_kill_stuck_data : int -> int array Effect.t
    15→      (* wallnum -> [is_d2, num_stuck, wallnum_0, objnum_0, obj_type_0, ...] = 98 ints *)
    16→  | Write_kill_stuck_objects : int array -> unit Effect.t
    17→      (* [new_num_stuck, n_matches, (slot_i, objnum_i, is_weapon_i) x n_matches] *)
    18→  | Flush_fcd_cache : unit -> unit Effect.t
    19→  | Fetch_seg_children_and_wall_nums : int -> int array Effect.t
    20→      (* segnum -> [children[0..5]; side_wall_nums[0..5]] = 12 ints *)
    21→  | Wall_set_flags : int * int -> unit Effect.t
    22→      (* (wall_num, flags_to_set) -> Walls[wall_num].flags |= flags *)
    23→  | Wall_clear_flags : int * int -> unit Effect.t
    24→      (* (wall_num, flags_to_clear) -> Walls[wall_num].flags &= ~flags *)
    25→
    26→(* reset_walls: Tidy up Walls array for load/save purposes.
    27→   Resets all wall entries from Num_walls to MAX_WALLS-1 to defaults.
    28→   Ported from wall.cpp (identical in D1 and D2). *)
    29→let reset_walls () =
    30→  (* Fetch [num_walls; is_d2] from C *)
    31→  let info = Effect.perform (Fetch_reset_walls_info ()) in
    32→  let num_walls = info.(0) in
    33→  let is_d2 = info.(1) in
    34→  if num_walls < 0 then
    35→    Printf.eprintf "Illegal Num_walls\n"
    36→  else begin
    37→    let max_walls = if is_d2 <> 0 then max_walls_d2 else max_walls_d1 in
    38→    (* Tell C to reset walls[num_walls .. max_walls-1] to defaults:
    39→       type=WALL_NORMAL(0), flags=0, hps=0, trigger=-1, clip_num=-1 *)
    40→    Effect.perform (Write_reset_walls [| num_walls; max_walls |])
    41→  end
    42→;;
    43→
    44→(* Constants for kill_stuck_objects *)
    45→let max_stuck_objects = 32
    46→let obj_weapon = 5
    47→let f1_0 = 0x10000
    48→
    49→(* kill_stuck_objects: Door with wall index wallnum is opening, kill all objects stuck in it.
    50→   Ported from main_d1/wall.cpp and main_d2/wall.cpp.
    51→   D1: lifeleft = F1_0/4, no flush_fcd_cache
    52→   D2: lifeleft = F1_0/8, flush_fcd_cache at end *)
    53→let kill_stuck_objects ~wallnum =
    54→  (* Fetch stuck objects data:
    55→     [is_d2, num_stuck, wallnum_0, objnum_0, obj_type_0, ...] *)
    56→  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_kill_stuck_data wallnum) in
    57→  let is_d2 = data.(0) <> 0 in
    58→  let num_stuck = data.(1) in
    59→  if num_stuck = 0 then ()
    60→  else begin
    61→  (* Num_stuck_objects = 0 at start, then recount *)
    62→  let new_num_stuck = ref 0 in
    63→  let matches = Array.create ~len:(max_stuck_objects * 3) 0 in
    64→  let n_matches = ref 0 in
    65→  for i = 0 to max_stuck_objects - 1 do
    66→    let base = 2 + i * 3 in
    67→    let wn = data.(base) in
    68→    let objnum = data.(base + 1) in
    69→    let obj_type = data.(base + 2) in
    70→    if wn = wallnum then begin
    71→      let is_weapon = if obj_type = obj_weapon then 1 else 0 in
    72→      if obj_type <> obj_weapon then
    73→        Printf.eprintf "Warning: Stuck object of type %d, expected to be of type %d, see wall.c\n"
    74→          obj_type obj_weapon;
    75→      matches.(!n_matches * 3) <- i;
    76→      matches.(!n_matches * 3 + 1) <- objnum;
    77→      matches.(!n_matches * 3 + 2) <- is_weapon;
    78→      n_matches := !n_matches + 1
    79→    end else if wn <> -1 then
    80→      new_num_stuck := !new_num_stuck + 1
    81→  done;
    82→  (* Write back: [new_num_stuck, n_matches, (slot_i, objnum_i, is_weapon_i) x n_matches] *)
    83→  let result = Array.create ~len:(2 + !n_matches * 3) 0 in
    84→  result.(0) <- !new_num_stuck;
    85→  result.(1) <- !n_matches;
    86→  for j = 0 to !n_matches - 1 do
    87→    result.(2 + j * 3) <- matches.(j * 3);
    88→    result.(2 + j * 3 + 1) <- matches.(j * 3 + 1);
    89→    result.(2 + j * 3 + 2) <- matches.(j * 3 + 2)
    90→  done;
    91→  Effect.perform (Write_kill_stuck_objects result);
    92→  (* D2 calls flush_fcd_cache; D1 does not *)
    93→  if is_d2 then
    94→    Effect.perform (Flush_fcd_cache ())
    95→  end
    96→;;
    97→
    98→(* WALL_ILLUSION_OFF = 32 *)
    99→let wall_illusion_off_flag = 32
   100→
   101→(* wall_illusion_off: Turn off an illusionary wall.
   102→   Ported from wall.cpp (identical in D1 and D2). *)
   103→let wall_illusion_off ~segnum ~side =
   104→  let seg_data = Effect.perform (Fetch_seg_children_and_wall_nums segnum) in
   105→  let child_seg = seg_data.(side) in
   106→  let child_data = Effect.perform (Fetch_seg_children_and_wall_nums child_seg) in
   107→  let child_children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> child_data.(i)) in
   108→  let cside = Ox_gameseg.find_connect_side ~children:child_children ~base_seg_num:segnum in
   109→  assert (cside <> -1);
   110→  let wall_num = seg_data.(6 + side) in
   111→  if wall_num = -1 then ()
   112→  else begin
   113→    let cwall_num = child_data.(6 + cside) in
   114→    Effect.perform (Wall_set_flags (wall_num, wall_illusion_off_flag));
   115→    Effect.perform (Wall_set_flags (cwall_num, wall_illusion_off_flag))
   116→  end
   117→;;
   118→
   119→(* wall_illusion_on: Turn on an illusionary wall.
   120→   Ported from wall.cpp (identical in D1 and D2). *)
   121→let wall_illusion_on ~segnum ~side =
   122→  let seg_data = Effect.perform (Fetch_seg_children_and_wall_nums segnum) in
   123→  let child_seg = seg_data.(side) in
   124→  let child_data = Effect.perform (Fetch_seg_children_and_wall_nums child_seg) in
   125→  let child_children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> child_data.(i)) in
   126→  let cside = Ox_gameseg.find_connect_side ~children:child_children ~base_seg_num:segnum in
   127→  assert (cside <> -1);
   128→  let wall_num = seg_data.(6 + side) in
   129→  if wall_num = -1 then ()
   130→  else begin
   131→    let cwall_num = child_data.(6 + cside) in
   132→    Effect.perform (Wall_clear_flags (wall_num, wall_illusion_off_flag));
   133→    Effect.perform (Wall_clear_flags (cwall_num, wall_illusion_off_flag))
   134→  end
   135→;;
   136→

1→(* Bridge adapter for wall functions.
     2→   Uses algebraic effects: OCaml performs effects, C handler executes them. *)
     3→
     4→(* Effect externals *)
     5→external effect_fetch_reset_walls_info
     6→  :  unit
     7→  -> int array
     8→  = "cd_ox_effect_wall_fetch_reset_walls_info"
     9→
    10→external effect_write_reset_walls
    11→  :  int array
    12→  -> unit
    13→  = "cd_ox_effect_wall_write_reset_walls"
    14→
    15→external effect_fetch_kill_stuck_data
    16→  :  int
    17→  -> int array
    18→  = "cd_ox_effect_wall_fetch_kill_stuck_data"
    19→
    20→external effect_write_kill_stuck_objects
    21→  :  int array
    22→  -> unit
    23→  = "cd_ox_effect_wall_write_kill_stuck_objects"
    24→
    25→external effect_flush_fcd_cache
    26→  :  unit
    27→  -> unit
    28→  = "cd_ox_effect_wall_flush_fcd_cache"
    29→
    30→external effect_fetch_seg_children_and_wall_nums
    31→  :  int
    32→  -> int array
    33→  = "cd_ox_effect_wall_fetch_seg_children_and_wall_nums"
    34→
    35→external effect_wall_set_flags
    36→  :  int
    37→  -> int
    38→  -> unit
    39→  = "cd_ox_effect_wall_set_flags"
    40→
    41→external effect_wall_clear_flags
    42→  :  int
    43→  -> int
    44→  -> unit
    45→  = "cd_ox_effect_wall_clear_flags"
    46→
    47→(* Effect handler *)
    48→let effc (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) : ((a, 'b) Effect.Deep.continuation -> 'b) option =
    49→  match eff with
    50→  | Ox_wall.Fetch_reset_walls_info () ->
    51→    Some (fun k ->
    52→      let data = effect_fetch_reset_walls_info () in
    53→      Effect.Deep.continue k data)
    54→  | Ox_wall.Write_reset_walls packed ->
    55→    Some (fun k ->
    56→      effect_write_reset_walls packed;
    57→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
    58→  | Ox_wall.Fetch_kill_stuck_data wallnum ->
    59→    Some (fun k ->
    60→      let data = effect_fetch_kill_stuck_data wallnum in
    61→      Effect.Deep.continue k data)
    62→  | Ox_wall.Write_kill_stuck_objects packed ->
    63→    Some (fun k ->
    64→      effect_write_kill_stuck_objects packed;
    65→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
    66→  | Ox_wall.Flush_fcd_cache () ->
    67→    Some (fun k ->
    68→      effect_flush_fcd_cache ();
    69→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
    70→  | Ox_wall.Fetch_seg_children_and_wall_nums segnum ->
    71→    Some (fun k ->
    72→      let data = effect_fetch_seg_children_and_wall_nums segnum in
    73→      Effect.Deep.continue k data)
    74→  | Ox_wall.Wall_set_flags (wall_num, flags) ->
    75→    Some (fun k ->
    76→      effect_wall_set_flags wall_num flags;
    77→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
    78→  | Ox_wall.Wall_clear_flags (wall_num, flags) ->
    79→    Some (fun k ->
    80→      effect_wall_clear_flags wall_num flags;
    81→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
    82→  | _ -> None
    83→;;
    84→
    85→(* Bridge entry point called from C via caml_callback *)
    86→let cd_reset_walls () =
    87→  Effect.Deep.match_with
    88→    (fun () -> Ox_wall.reset_walls ())
    89→    ()
    90→    { retc = (fun () -> ())
    91→    ; exnc = (fun e ->
    92→        Printf.eprintf "[OX] reset_walls exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string e);
    93→        Out_channel.flush stderr)
    94→    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> effc eff)
    95→    }
    96→;;
    97→
    98→let cd_kill_stuck_objects wallnum =
    99→  Effect.Deep.match_with
   100→    (fun () -> Ox_wall.kill_stuck_objects ~wallnum)
   101→    ()
   102→    { retc = (fun () -> ())
   103→    ; exnc = (fun e ->
   104→        Printf.eprintf "[OX] kill_stuck_objects exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string e);
   105→        Out_channel.flush stderr)
   106→    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> effc eff)
   107→    }
   108→;;
   109→
   110→let cd_wall_illusion_off segnum side =
   111→  Effect.Deep.match_with
   112→    (fun () -> Ox_wall.wall_illusion_off ~segnum ~side)
   113→    ()
   114→    { retc = (fun () -> ())
   115→    ; exnc = (fun e ->
   116→        Printf.eprintf "[OX] wall_illusion_off exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string e);
   117→        Out_channel.flush stderr)
   118→    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> effc eff)
   119→    }
   120→;;
   121→
   122→let cd_wall_illusion_on segnum side =
   123→  Effect.Deep.match_with
   124→    (fun () -> Ox_wall.wall_illusion_on ~segnum ~side)
   125→    ()
   126→    { retc = (fun () -> ())
   127→    ; exnc = (fun e ->
   128→        Printf.eprintf "[OX] wall_illusion_on exception: %s\n" (Exn.to_string e);
   129→        Out_channel.flush stderr)
   130→    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) -> effc eff)
   131→    }
   132→;;
   133→
   134→let register_callbacks () =
   135→  Callback.register "cd_reset_walls" cd_reset_walls;
   136→  Callback.register "cd_kill_stuck_objects" cd_kill_stuck_objects;
   137→  Callback.register "cd_wall_illusion_off" cd_wall_illusion_off;
   138→  Callback.register "cd_wall_illusion_on" cd_wall_illusion_on
   139→;;

     4→type _ Effect.t += Fetch_segment_data : int -> int array Effect.t

     6→let side_to_verts =
     7→  [| [| 7; 6; 2; 3 |]
     8→   ; [| 0; 4; 7; 3 |]
     9→   ; [| 0; 1; 5; 4 |]
    10→   ; [| 2; 6; 5; 1 |]
    11→   ; [| 4; 5; 6; 7 |]

    16→let _side_is_quad = 1 [@@warning "-32"]

    18→let _side_is_tri_13 = 3 [@@warning "-32"]
    19→let _wleft = 0 [@@warning "-32"]
    20→let wtop = 1
    21→let _wright = 2 [@@warning "-32"]
    22→let wbottom = 3
    23→let wback = 4

    27→type vec3 = int * int * int
    28→
    29→let compute_center_point_on_side ~(v0 : vec3) ~(v1 : vec3) ~(v2 : vec3) ~(v3 : vec3)
    30→  : vec3
    31→  =
    32→  (* C uses vm_vec_add2 loop + vm_vec_scale(F1_0/4), NOT vm_vec_avg4.
    33→     fixmul rounds toward -inf for negatives, / 4 rounds toward zero. *)
    34→  let open Ox_math in
    35→  let s = vm_vec_add2 ~a:(vm_vec_add2 ~a:(vm_vec_add2 ~a:v0 ~b:v1) ~b:v2) ~b:v3 in
    36→  let sx, sy, sz = s in
    37→  let scale = 0x10000 / 4 in
    38→  fixmul ~a:sx ~b:scale, fixmul ~a:sy ~b:scale, fixmul ~a:sz ~b:scale
    39→;;
    40→
    41→let compute_segment_center
    42→      ~(v0 : vec3)
    43→      ~(v1 : vec3)
    44→      ~(v2 : vec3)
    45→      ~(v3 : vec3)
    46→      ~(v4 : vec3)
    47→      ~(v5 : vec3)
    48→      ~(v6 : vec3)
    49→      ~(v7 : vec3)
    50→  : vec3
    51→  =
    52→  let open Ox_math in
    53→  let s =
    54→    vm_vec_add2
    55→      ~a:
    56→        (vm_vec_add2
    57→           ~a:
    58→             (vm_vec_add2
    59→                ~a:
    60→                  (vm_vec_add2
    61→                     ~a:
    62→                       (vm_vec_add2
    63→                          ~a:(vm_vec_add2 ~a:(vm_vec_add2 ~a:v0 ~b:v1) ~b:v2)
    64→                          ~b:v3)
    65→                     ~b:v4)
    66→                ~b:v5)
    67→           ~b:v6)
    68→      ~b:v7
    69→  in
    70→  let sx, sy, sz = s in
    71→  let scale = 0x10000 / 8 in
    72→  Ox_math.fixmul ~a:sx ~b:scale, Ox_math.fixmul ~a:sy ~b:scale, Ox_math.fixmul ~a:sz ~b:scale

    74→
    75→let get_verts_for_normal ~va ~vb ~vc ~vd =
    76→  let v = [| va; vb; vc; vd |] in
    77→  let w = [| 0; 1; 2; 3 |] in
    78→  for i = 1 to 3 do
    79→    for j = 0 to i - 1 do
    80→      if v.(j) > v.(i)
    81→      then (
    82→        let t = v.(j) in
    83→        v.(j) <- v.(i);
    84→        v.(i) <- t;
    85→        let t = w.(j) in
    86→        w.(j) <- w.(i);
    87→        w.(i) <- t)
    88→    done
    89→  done;
    90→  let negate_flag =
    91→    if (w.(0) + 3) mod 4 = w.(1) || (w.(1) + 3) mod 4 = w.(2) then 1 else 0
    92→  in
    93→  v.(0), v.(1), v.(2), v.(3), negate_flag

    96→let create_abs_vertex_lists ~side_type ~(seg_verts : int array) ~sidenum =
    97→  let sv = side_to_verts.(sidenum) in
    98→  match side_type with
    99→  | 1 ->
   100→    (* SIDE_IS_QUAD *)
   101→    let vertices =
   102→      [| seg_verts.(sv.(0))
   103→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(1))
   104→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(2))
   105→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(3))
   106→       ; 0
   107→       ; 0
   108→      |]
   109→    in
   110→    1, vertices
   111→  | 2 ->
   112→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_02 *)
   113→    let vertices =
   114→      [| seg_verts.(sv.(0))
   115→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(1))
   116→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(2))
   117→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(2))
   118→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(3))
   119→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(0))
   120→      |]
   121→    in
   122→    2, vertices
   123→  | 3 ->
   124→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_13 *)
   125→    let vertices =
   126→      [| seg_verts.(sv.(3))
   127→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(0))
   128→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(1))
   129→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(1))
   130→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(2))
   131→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(3))
   132→      |]
   133→    in
   134→    2, vertices
   135→  | _ -> failwith (Printf.sprintf "Illegal side type %d in create_abs_vertex_lists (sidenum=%d)" side_type sidenum)
   136→;;
   137→
   138→(* Lookup a vertex position by absolute vertex index.
   139→   seg_verts maps relative index 0..7 to absolute vertex indices.
   140→   seg_vert_positions.(i) is the position of seg_verts.(i). *)
   141→let lookup_vpos ~(seg_verts : int array) ~(seg_vert_positions : vec3 array) ~abs_idx =
   142→  let rec find i =
   143→    if i >= 8
   144→    then 0, 0, 0 (* should not happen *)
   145→    else if seg_verts.(i) = abs_idx
   146→    then seg_vert_positions.(i)
   147→    else find (i + 1)
   148→  in
   149→  find 0
   150→;;
   151→
   152→let get_seg_masks
   153→      ~checkp:(cpx, cpy, cpz)
   154→      ~rad
   155→      ~(seg_verts : int array)
   156→      ~(side_types : int array)
   157→      ~(normals : vec3 array)
   158→      ~(seg_vert_positions : vec3 array)
   159→  =
   160→  let vpos abs_idx = lookup_vpos ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions ~abs_idx in
   161→  let facemask = ref 0 in
   162→  let sidemask = ref 0 in
   163→  let centermask = ref 0 in
   164→  let facebit = ref 1 in
   165→  let sidebit = ref 1 in
   166→  for sn = 0 to 5 do
   167→    let side_type = side_types.(sn) in
   168→    let n0 = normals.(sn * 2) in
   169→    let n1 = normals.((sn * 2) + 1) in
   170→    let num_faces, vertex_list =
   171→      create_abs_vertex_lists ~side_type ~seg_verts ~sidenum:sn
   172→    in
   173→    if num_faces = 2
   174→    then (
   175→      let vertnum = min vertex_list.(0) vertex_list.(2) in
   176→      let planep = vpos vertnum in
   177→      let dist =
   178→        if vertex_list.(4) < vertex_list.(1)
   179→        then Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(vpos vertex_list.(4)) ~norm:n0 ~planep
   180→        else Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(vpos vertex_list.(1)) ~norm:n1 ~planep
   181→      in
   182→      let side_pokes_out = dist > plane_dist_tolerance in
   183→      let side_count = ref 0 in
   184→      let center_count = ref 0 in
   185→      for fn = 0 to 1 do
   186→        let norm = if fn = 0 then n0 else n1 in
   187→        let d = Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(cpx, cpy, cpz) ~norm ~planep in
   188→        if d < -plane_dist_tolerance then incr center_count;
   189→        if d - rad < -plane_dist_tolerance
   190→        then (
   191→          facemask := !facemask lor !facebit;
   192→          incr side_count);
   193→        facebit := !facebit lsl 1
   194→      done;
   195→      if not side_pokes_out
   196→      then (
   197→        if !side_count = 2 then sidemask := !sidemask lor !sidebit;
   198→        if !center_count = 2 then centermask := !centermask lor !sidebit)
   199→      else (
   200→        if !side_count > 0 then sidemask := !sidemask lor !sidebit;
   201→        if !center_count > 0 then centermask := !centermask lor !sidebit))
   202→    else (
   203→      let vertnum = ref vertex_list.(0) in
   204→      for i = 1 to 3 do
   205→        if vertex_list.(i) < !vertnum then vertnum := vertex_list.(i)
   206→      done;
   207→      let d =
   208→        Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(cpx, cpy, cpz) ~norm:n0 ~planep:(vpos !vertnum)
   209→      in
   210→      if d < -plane_dist_tolerance then centermask := !centermask lor !sidebit;
   211→      if d - rad < -plane_dist_tolerance
   212→      then (
   213→        facemask := !facemask lor !facebit;
   214→        sidemask := !sidemask lor !sidebit);
   215→      facebit := !facebit lsl 2);
   216→    sidebit := !sidebit lsl 1
   217→  done;
   218→  !facemask, !sidemask, !centermask
   219→;;
   220→
   221→let get_side_dists
   222→      ~checkp:(cpx, cpy, cpz)
   223→      ~(seg_verts : int array)
   224→      ~(side_types : int array)
   225→      ~(normals : vec3 array)
   226→      ~(seg_vert_positions : vec3 array)
   227→  =
   228→  let vpos abs_idx = lookup_vpos ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions ~abs_idx in
   229→  let mask = ref 0 in
   230→  let side_dists = Array.create ~len:6 0 in
   231→  let facebit = ref 1 in
   232→  let sidebit = ref 1 in
   233→  for sn = 0 to 5 do
   234→    let side_type = side_types.(sn) in
   235→    let n0 = normals.(sn * 2) in
   236→    let n1 = normals.((sn * 2) + 1) in
   237→    let num_faces, vertex_list =
   238→      create_abs_vertex_lists ~side_type ~seg_verts ~sidenum:sn
   239→    in
   240→    side_dists.(sn) <- 0;
   241→    if num_faces = 2
   242→    then (
   243→      let vertnum = min vertex_list.(0) vertex_list.(2) in
   244→      let planep = vpos vertnum in
   245→      let dist =
   246→        if vertex_list.(4) < vertex_list.(1)
   247→        then Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(vpos vertex_list.(4)) ~norm:n0 ~planep
   248→        else Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(vpos vertex_list.(1)) ~norm:n1 ~planep
   249→      in
   250→      let side_pokes_out = dist > plane_dist_tolerance in
   251→      let center_count = ref 0 in
   252→      for fn = 0 to 1 do
   253→        let norm = if fn = 0 then n0 else n1 in
   254→        let d = Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(cpx, cpy, cpz) ~norm ~planep in
   255→        if d < -plane_dist_tolerance
   256→        then (
   257→          incr center_count;
   258→          side_dists.(sn) <- side_dists.(sn) + d);
   259→        facebit := !facebit lsl 1
   260→      done;
   261→      if not side_pokes_out
   262→      then (
   263→        if !center_count = 2
   264→        then (
   265→          mask := !mask lor !sidebit;
   266→          side_dists.(sn) <- side_dists.(sn) / 2))
   267→      else if !center_count > 0
   268→      then (
   269→        mask := !mask lor !sidebit;
   270→        if !center_count = 2 then side_dists.(sn) <- side_dists.(sn) / 2))
   271→    else (
   272→      let vertnum = ref vertex_list.(0) in
   273→      for i = 1 to 3 do
   274→        if vertex_list.(i) < !vertnum then vertnum := vertex_list.(i)
   275→      done;
   276→      let d =
   277→        Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(cpx, cpy, cpz) ~norm:n0 ~planep:(vpos !vertnum)
   278→      in
   279→      if d < -plane_dist_tolerance
   280→      then (
   281→        mask := !mask lor !sidebit;
   282→        side_dists.(sn) <- d);
   283→      facebit := !facebit lsl 2);
   284→    sidebit := !sidebit lsl 1
   285→  done;
   286→  !mask, side_dists
   287→;;
   288→
   289→let extract_vector_from_segment ~(verts : vec3 array) ~start_side ~end_side =
   290→  let sv_start = side_to_verts.(start_side) in
   291→  let sv_end = side_to_verts.(end_side) in
   292→  let vs = ref (0, 0, 0) in
   293→  let ve = ref (0, 0, 0) in
   294→  for i = 0 to 3 do
   295→    vs := Ox_math.vm_vec_add2 ~a:!vs ~b:verts.(sv_start.(i));
   296→    ve := Ox_math.vm_vec_add2 ~a:!ve ~b:verts.(sv_end.(i))
   297→  done;
   298→  let diff = Ox_math.vm_vec_sub ~a:!ve ~b:!vs in
   299→  Ox_math.vm_vec_scale ~v:diff ~k:(0x10000 / 4)
   300→;;
   301→
   302→let extract_orient_from_segment (verts : vec3 array) =
   303→  let fvec = extract_vector_from_segment ~verts ~start_side:wfront ~end_side:wback in
   304→  let uvec = extract_vector_from_segment ~verts ~start_side:wbottom ~end_side:wtop in
   305→  Ox_math.vm_vector_2_matrix ~fvec ~uvec:(Some uvec) ~rvec:None
   306→;;
   307→
   308→let max_sides_per_segment = 6
   309→
   310→(* find_connect_side: given a segment's children array and a base segment number,
   311→   return the side index that connects to the base segment, or -1 if none. *)
   312→let find_connect_side ~(children : int array) ~base_seg_num =
   313→  let rec loop s =
   314→    if s >= max_sides_per_segment
   315→    then -1
   316→    else if children.(s) = base_seg_num
   317→    then s
   318→    else loop (s + 1)
   319→  in
   320→  loop 0
   321→;;
   322→
   323→(* --- find_connected_distance ----------------------------------------- *)
   324→(* BFS to find distance between two points via segment connectivity.
   325→   Packed array layout:
   326→   Header (12 ints):
   327→     [0..2]  p0.x, p0.y, p0.z
   328→     [3]     seg0
   329→     [4..6]  p1.x, p1.y, p1.z
   330→     [7]     seg1
   331→     [8]     max_depth (-1 = unlimited)
   332→     [9]     wid_flag
   333→     [10]    n_segments
   334→     [11]    check_wid_on_adjacency (0=D1 skip, 1=D2 check)
   335→
   336→   Per-segment (15 ints each):
   337→     [0..5]   children[0..5]
   338→     [6..11]  wid[0..5] — pre-evaluated WALL_IS_DOORWAY results
   339→     [12..14] center.x, center.y, center.z — pre-computed segment center
   340→
   341→   Returns: [| distance (fix) or -1; connected_segment_distance |] *)
   342→
   343→let max_loc_point_segs = 64
   344→
   345→let find_connected_distance (arr : int array) =
   346→  let p0 = arr.(0), arr.(1), arr.(2) in
   347→  let seg0 = arr.(3) in
   348→  let p1 = arr.(4), arr.(5), arr.(6) in
   349→  let seg1 = arr.(7) in
   350→  let max_depth_raw = arr.(8) in
   351→  let wid_flag = arr.(9) in
   352→  let n_segments = arr.(10) in
   353→  let check_wid_adj = arr.(11) in
   354→  let header = 12 in
   355→  let per_seg = 15 in
   356→  let max_depth =
   357→    if max_depth_raw > max_loc_point_segs - 2
   358→    then max_loc_point_segs - 2
   359→    else max_depth_raw
   360→  in
   361→  let seg_children s i = arr.(header + (s * per_seg) + i) in
   362→  let seg_wid s i = arr.(header + (s * per_seg) + 6 + i) in
   363→  let seg_center s =
   364→    let base = header + (s * per_seg) + 12 in
   365→    arr.(base), arr.(base + 1), arr.(base + 2)
   366→  in
   367→  (* Quick return: same segment *)
   368→  if seg0 = seg1
   369→  then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; 0 |]
   370→  else (
   371→    (* Adjacency check *)
   372→    let conn_side = ref (-1) in
   373→    for side = 0 to 5 do
   374→      if
   375→        !conn_side = -1 && seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments && seg_children seg0 side = seg1
   376→      then conn_side := side
   377→    done;
   378→    if
   379→      !conn_side <> -1
   380→      && (check_wid_adj = 0
   381→          || (seg1 >= 0 && seg1 < n_segments && seg_wid seg1 !conn_side land wid_flag <> 0)
   382→         )
   383→    then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; 1 |]
   384→    else (
   385→      (* BFS *)
   386→      let visited = Array.create ~len:n_segments false in
   387→      let q_start = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
   388→      let q_end = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
   389→      let q_depth = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
   390→      let qtail = ref 0 in
   391→      let qhead = ref 0 in
   392→      let cur_seg = ref seg0 in
   393→      let cur_depth = ref 0 in
   394→      let found = ref false in
   395→      let failed = ref false in
   396→      if seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments then visited.(seg0) <- true;
   397→      while !cur_seg <> seg1 && not !failed do
   398→        let cs = !cur_seg in
   399→        if cs < 0 || cs >= n_segments
   400→        then failed := true
   401→        else (
   402→          for sidenum = 0 to 5 do
   403→            if (not !failed) && seg_wid cs sidenum land wid_flag <> 0
   404→            then (
   405→              let child = seg_children cs sidenum in
   406→              if child >= 0 && child < n_segments && not visited.(child)
   407→              then (
   408→                let qt = !qtail in
   409→                q_start.(qt) <- cs;
   410→                q_end.(qt) <- child;
   411→                visited.(child) <- true;
   412→                q_depth.(qt) <- !cur_depth + 1;
   413→                qtail := qt + 1;
   414→                if max_depth <> -1 && !cur_depth + 1 = max_depth
   415→                then failed := true
   416→                else if max_depth = -1 && child = seg1
   417→                then (
   418→                  found := true;
   419→                  cur_seg := seg1)))
   420→          done;
   421→          if (not !found) && not !failed
   422→          then
   423→            if !qhead >= !qtail
   424→            then failed := true
   425→            else (
   426→              cur_seg := q_end.(!qhead);
   427→              cur_depth := q_depth.(!qhead);
   428→              qhead := !qhead + 1))
   429→      done;
   430→      if !failed
   431→      then [| -1; 1000 |]
   432→      else (
   433→        (* Path reconstruction: trace back from seg1 to seg0 *)
   434→        let path_centers = Array.create ~len:max_loc_point_segs (0, 0, 0) in
   435→        let num_points = ref 0 in
   436→        (* Find last queue entry ending at seg1 *)
   437→        let qt = ref (!qtail - 1) in
   438→        while !qt >= 0 && q_end.(!qt) <> seg1 do
   439→          qt := !qt - 1
   440→        done;
   441→        if !qt < 0
   442→        then [| -1; 1000 |]
   443→        else (
   444→          (* Walk backwards through BFS tree *)
   445→          let done_ = ref false in
   446→          while !qt >= 0 && not !done_ do
   447→            let this_seg = q_end.(!qt) in
   448→            let parent_seg = q_start.(!qt) in
   449→            if !num_points < max_loc_point_segs
   450→            then (
   451→              path_centers.(!num_points) <- seg_center this_seg;
   452→              num_points := !num_points + 1);
   453→            if parent_seg = seg0
   454→            then done_ := true
   455→            else (
   456→              qt := !qt - 1;
   457→              while !qt >= 0 && q_end.(!qt) <> parent_seg do
   458→                qt := !qt - 1
   459→              done;
   460→              if !qt < 0 then done_ := true)
   461→          done;
   462→          if !qt < 0 && not !done_
   463→          then [| -1; 1000 |]
   464→          else (
   465→            (* Add seg0 center *)
   466→            if !num_points < max_loc_point_segs && seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments
   467→            then (
   468→              path_centers.(!num_points) <- seg_center seg0;
   469→              num_points := !num_points + 1);
   470→            let np = !num_points in
   471→            if np = 1
   472→            then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; np |]
   473→            else (
   474→              (* Sum distances along path:
   475→                 p1 -> path_centers[1] -> ... -> path_centers[np-2] -> p0 *)
   476→              let dist = ref 0 in
   477→              dist
   478→              := Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p1 ~b:path_centers.(1)
   479→                 + Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:path_centers.(np - 2);
   480→              for i = 1 to np - 3 do
   481→                dist
   482→                := !dist
   483→                   + Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:path_centers.(i) ~b:path_centers.(i + 1)
   484→              done;
   485→              [| !dist; np |]))))))
   486→;;
   487→
   488→let matrix_precision = 9
   489→let matrix_max = 0x7f
   490→let relpos_precision = 10
   491→let vel_precision = 12
   492→
   493→let convert_to_byte f =
   494→  if f >= 0x00010000
   495→  then matrix_max
   496→  else if f <= -0x00010000
   497→  then -matrix_max
   498→  else f asr matrix_precision
   499→;;
   500→
   501→(* create_shortpos: compress an object's orient/pos/vel into a shortpos.
   502→   orient: 9 ints (rx,ux,fx,ry,uy,fy,rz,uz,fz)
   503→   pos: (x,y,z), vel: (x,y,z), seg_vert0: (x,y,z) position of segment vertex 0
   504→   segnum: object's segment number
   505→   Returns: (bytemat[9], xo, yo, zo, segment, velx, vely, velz) *)
   506→let create_shortpos
   507→      ~(orient : int array)
   508→      ~(pos : vec3)
   509→      ~(vel : vec3)
   510→      ~(seg_vert0 : vec3)
   511→      ~segnum
   512→  =
   513→  let bytemat = Array.init 9 ~f:(fun i -> convert_to_byte orient.(i)) in
   514→  let px, py, pz = pos in
   515→  let v0x, v0y, v0z = seg_vert0 in
   516→  let xo = (px - v0x) asr relpos_precision in
   517→  let yo = (py - v0y) asr relpos_precision in
   518→  let zo = (pz - v0z) asr relpos_precision in
   519→  let vx, vy, vz = vel in
   520→  let velx = vx asr vel_precision in
   521→  let vely = vy asr vel_precision in
   522→  let velz = vz asr vel_precision in
   523→  bytemat, xo, yo, zo, segnum, velx, vely, velz
   524→;;
   525→
   526→(* extract_shortpos: decompress a shortpos back to orient/pos/vel.
   527→   bytemat: 9 ints, xo/yo/zo: relative position shorts,
   528→   seg_vert0: (x,y,z), velx/vely/velz: velocity shorts
   529→   Returns: (orient[9], pos(x,y,z), vel(x,y,z)) *)
   530→let extract_shortpos
   531→      ~(bytemat : int array)
   532→      ~xo
   533→      ~yo
   534→      ~zo
   535→      ~(seg_vert0 : vec3)
   536→      ~velx
   537→      ~vely
   538→      ~velz
   539→  =
   540→  let orient = Array.init 9 ~f:(fun i -> bytemat.(i) lsl matrix_precision) in
   541→  let v0x, v0y, v0z = seg_vert0 in
   542→  let pos =
   543→    ( (xo lsl relpos_precision) + v0x
   544→    , (yo lsl relpos_precision) + v0y
   545→    , (zo lsl relpos_precision) + v0z )
   546→  in
   547→  let vel = velx lsl vel_precision, vely lsl vel_precision, velz lsl vel_precision in
   548→  orient, pos, vel
   549→;;
   550→
   551→(* --- create_walls_on_side --------------------------------------------- *)
   552→(* Determine side type (quad or triangulated) and compute face normals.
   553→   Combines C functions: create_walls_on_side, add_side_as_quad, add_side_as_2_triangles.
   554→   v0..v3: 4 vertex positions (as vec3), vi0..vi3: their absolute vertex indices,
   555→   has_child: whether there's a child segment on this side.
   556→   Returns (side_type, normal0, normal1). *)
   557→
   558→let side_is_quad = 1
   559→let side_is_tri_02 = 2
   560→let side_is_tri_13 = 3
   561→let plane_dist_tolerance = 250
   562→let vec_negate (x, y, z) = -x, -y, -z
   563→
   564→(* Helper: look up vertex position by absolute index from our 4 known vertices *)
   565→let make_pos_lookup ~v0 ~vi0 ~v1 ~vi1 ~v2 ~vi2 ~v3 ~vi3 =
   566→  fun vi ->
   567→  if vi = vi0
   568→  then v0
   569→  else if vi = vi1
   570→  then v1
   571→  else if vi = vi2
   572→  then v2
   573→  else if vi = vi3
   574→  then v3
   575→  else failwith "create_walls_on_side: unknown vertex index"
   576→;;
   577→
   578→(* add_side_as_2_triangles logic: determine triangulation and compute normals *)
   579→let triangulate_side ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child =
   580→  let pos_of = make_pos_lookup ~v0 ~vi0 ~v1 ~vi1 ~v2 ~vi2 ~v3 ~vi3 in
   581→  if not has_child
   582→  then (
   583→    (* Non-wall case: use dot product to choose triangulation *)
   584→    let norm = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v0 ~p1:v1 ~p2:v2 in
   585→    let vec_13 = Ox_math.vm_vec_sub ~a:v3 ~b:v1 in
   586→    let dot = Ox_math.vm_vec_dotprod ~a:norm ~b:vec_13 in
   587→    if dot >= 0
   588→    then (
   589→      let n0 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v0 ~p1:v1 ~p2:v2 in
   590→      let n1 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v0 ~p1:v2 ~p2:v3 in
   591→      side_is_tri_02, n0, n1)
   592→    else (
   593→      let n0 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v0 ~p1:v1 ~p2:v3 in
   594→      let n1 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v1 ~p1:v2 ~p2:v3 in
   595→      side_is_tri_13, n0, n1))
   596→  else (
   597→    (* Wall case: use get_verts_for_normal for consistent normals *)
   598→    let vs0, _vs1, _vs2, _vs3, _nf =
   599→      get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi1 ~vc:vi2 ~vd:vi3
   600→    in
   601→    if vs0 = vi0 || vs0 = vi2
   602→    then (
   603→      (* TRI_02 *)
   604→      let s0, s1, s2, _, nf0 = get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi1 ~vc:vi2 ~vd:32767 in
   605→      let n0 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of s0) ~p1:(pos_of s1) ~p2:(pos_of s2) in
   606→      let n0 = if nf0 <> 0 then vec_negate n0 else n0 in
   607→      let s0, s1, s2, _, nf1 = get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi2 ~vc:vi3 ~vd:32767 in
   608→      let n1 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of s0) ~p1:(pos_of s1) ~p2:(pos_of s2) in
   609→      let n1 = if nf1 <> 0 then vec_negate n1 else n1 in
   610→      side_is_tri_02, n0, n1)
   611→    else (
   612→      (* TRI_13 *)
   613→      let s0, s1, s2, _, nf0 = get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi1 ~vc:vi3 ~vd:32767 in
   614→      let n0 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of s0) ~p1:(pos_of s1) ~p2:(pos_of s2) in
   615→      let n0 = if nf0 <> 0 then vec_negate n0 else n0 in
   616→      let s0, s1, s2, _, nf1 = get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi1 ~vb:vi2 ~vc:vi3 ~vd:32767 in
   617→      let n1 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of s0) ~p1:(pos_of s1) ~p2:(pos_of s2) in
   618→      let n1 = if nf1 <> 0 then vec_negate n1 else n1 in
   619→      side_is_tri_13, n0, n1))
   620→;;
   621→
   622→let create_walls_on_side ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child =
   623→  let pos_of = make_pos_lookup ~v0 ~vi0 ~v1 ~vi1 ~v2 ~vi2 ~v3 ~vi3 in
   624→  (* Step 1: Sort vertices, compute plane normal and planarity *)
   625→  let vm0, vm1, vm2, vm3, negate_flag =
   626→    get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi1 ~vc:vi2 ~vd:vi3
   627→  in
   628→  let vn = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of vm0) ~p1:(pos_of vm1) ~p2:(pos_of vm2) in
   629→  let dist =
   630→    abs (Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(pos_of vm3) ~norm:vn ~planep:(pos_of vm0))
   631→  in
   632→  let vn = if negate_flag <> 0 then vec_negate vn else vn in
   633→  (* Step 2: If planar, return as quad *)
   634→  if dist <= plane_dist_tolerance
   635→  then side_is_quad, vn, vn
   636→  else (
   637→    (* Step 3: Triangulate *)
   638→    let side_type, n0, n1 =
   639→      triangulate_side ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child
   640→    in
   641→    (* Step 4: De-triangulation check *)
   642→    (* Build vertex list for the computed triangulation *)
   643→    let vl =
   644→      if side_type = side_is_tri_02
   645→      then [| vi0; vi1; vi2; vi2; vi3; vi0 |]
   646→      else [| vi3; vi0; vi1; vi1; vi2; vi3 |]
   647→    in
   648→    let vertnum = min vl.(0) vl.(2) in
   649→    let dist0 =
   650→      Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(pos_of vl.(1)) ~norm:n1 ~planep:(pos_of vertnum)
   651→    in
   652→    let dist1 =
   653→      Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(pos_of vl.(4)) ~norm:n0 ~planep:(pos_of vertnum)
   654→    in
   655→    let sign v =
   656→      if v > plane_dist_tolerance
   657→      then 1
   658→      else if v < -(plane_dist_tolerance + 1)
   659→      then -1
   660→      else 0
   661→    in
   662→    let s0 = sign dist0 in
   663→    let s1 = sign dist1 in
   664→    if s0 = 0 || s1 = 0 || s0 <> s1
   665→    then (* De-triangulate back to quad *)
   666→      side_is_quad, vn, vn
   667→    else side_type, n0, n1)
   668→;;
   669→
   670→(* --- check_norms ------------------------------------------------------ *)
   671→(* Check if two face normals are consistent (should be negations of each other).
   672→   Returns 1 if mismatch, 0 if OK. *)
   673→let check_norms ~(n0 : vec3) ~(n1 : vec3) =
   674→  let n0x, n0y, n0z = n0 in
   675→  let n1x, n1y, n1z = n1 in
   676→  if n0x <> -n1x || n0y <> -n1y || n0z <> -n1z then 1 else 0
   677→;;
   678→
   679→(* --- create_all_vertex_lists ------------------------------------------ *)
   680→(* Like create_abs_vertex_lists but returns relative vertex indices (0..7 within segment)
   681→   instead of absolute vertex indices. Takes side_type and sidenum. *)
   682→let create_all_vertex_lists ~side_type ~sidenum =
   683→  let sv = side_to_verts.(sidenum) in
   684→  match side_type with
   685→  | 1 ->
   686→    (* SIDE_IS_QUAD *)
   687→    1, [| sv.(0); sv.(1); sv.(2); sv.(3); 0; 0 |]
   688→  | 2 ->
   689→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_02 *)
   690→    2, [| sv.(0); sv.(1); sv.(2); sv.(2); sv.(3); sv.(0) |]
   691→  | 3 ->
   692→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_13 *)
   693→    2, [| sv.(3); sv.(0); sv.(1); sv.(1); sv.(2); sv.(3) |]
   694→  | _ -> failwith (Printf.sprintf "Illegal side type %d" side_type)
   695→;;
   696→
   697→(* --- create_all_vertnum_lists ----------------------------------------- *)
   698→(* Returns vertex number indices (0..3 within side) for each face of a side.
   699→   Only depends on side_type. *)
   700→let create_all_vertnum_lists ~side_type =
   701→  match side_type with
   702→  | 1 ->
   703→    (* SIDE_IS_QUAD *)
   704→    1, [| 0; 1; 2; 3; 0; 0 |]
   705→  | 2 ->
   706→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_02 *)
   707→    2, [| 0; 1; 2; 2; 3; 0 |]
   708→  | 3 ->
   709→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_13 *)
   710→    2, [| 3; 0; 1; 1; 2; 3 |]
   711→  | _ -> failwith (Printf.sprintf "Illegal side type %d" side_type)
   712→;;
   713→
   714→(* --- trace_segs + find_point_seg ------------------------------------- *)
   715→(* Packed array layout (same 80-int-per-segment as sphere_intersects_wall):
   716→   Header (6 ints):
   717→     [0..2] p.x, p.y, p.z
   718→     [3]    segnum (hint, -1 if unknown)
   719→     [4]    n_segments
   720→     [5]    doing_lighting_hack (D1: always 0; D2: runtime flag)
   721→
   722→   Per-segment (80 ints each):
   723→     [0..5]   children[0..5]
   724→     [6..11]  side_types[0..5]
   725→     [12..19] seg_verts[0..7]
   726→     [20..55] normals: side 0..5, normals[0].xyz then normals[1].xyz
   727→     [56..79] vertex_positions: vert 0..7, xyz
   728→
   729→   Returns segment number or -1. *)
   730→
   731→let find_point_seg (arr : int array) =
   732→  let px = arr.(0) in
   733→  let py = arr.(1) in
   734→  let pz = arr.(2) in
   735→  let segnum = arr.(3) in
   736→  let n_segments = arr.(4) in
   737→  let doing_lighting_hack = arr.(5) in
   738→  let header = 6 in
   739→  let per_seg = 80 in
   740→  let get_seg_base s = header + (s * per_seg) in
   741→  let unpack_seg s =
   742→    let base = get_seg_base s in
   743→    let children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(base + i)) in
   744→    let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(base + 6 + i)) in
   745→    let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(base + 12 + i)) in
   746→    let normals =
   747→      Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
   748→        let off = base + 20 + (i * 3) in
   749→        arr.(off), arr.(off + 1), arr.(off + 2))
   750→    in
   751→    let seg_vert_positions =
   752→      Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   753→        let off = base + 56 + (i * 3) in
   754→        arr.(off), arr.(off + 1), arr.(off + 2))
   755→    in
   756→    children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions
   757→  in
   758→  (* trace_segs: recursively find segment containing point by following
   759→     the child side with the most-negative distance. *)
   760→  let rec trace s iterations =
   761→    if s < 0 || s >= n_segments
   762→    then -1
   763→    else if iterations > 1024
   764→    then s
   765→    else (
   766→      let children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = unpack_seg s in
   767→      let centermask, side_dists =
   768→        get_side_dists
   769→          ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   770→          ~seg_verts
   771→          ~side_types
   772→          ~normals
   773→          ~seg_vert_positions
   774→      in
   775→      if centermask = 0
   776→      then s
   777→      else (
   778→        (* Make a mutable copy of side_dists so we can zero out tried sides *)
   779→        let sd = Array.copy side_dists in
   780→        let result = ref (-1) in
   781→        let continue_ = ref true in
   782→        while !continue_ do
   783→          let biggest_side = ref (-1) in
   784→          let biggest_val = ref 0 in
   785→          let bit = ref 1 in
   786→          for sn = 0 to 5 do
   787→            if centermask land !bit <> 0 && children.(sn) > -1 && sd.(sn) < !biggest_val
   788→            then (
   789→              biggest_val := sd.(sn);
   790→              biggest_side := sn);
   791→            bit := !bit lsl 1
   792→          done;
   793→          if !biggest_side <> -1
   794→          then (
   795→            sd.(!biggest_side) <- 0;
   796→            let check = trace children.(!biggest_side) (iterations + 1) in
   797→            if check <> -1
   798→            then (
   799→              result := check;
   800→              continue_ := false))
   801→          else continue_ := false
   802→        done;
   803→        !result))
   804→  in
   805→  (* find_point_seg: try trace_segs from hint, fall back to exhaustive scan *)
   806→  if segnum >= 0 && segnum < n_segments
   807→  then (
   808→    let newseg = trace segnum 0 in
   809→    if newseg <> -1
   810→    then newseg
   811→    else if doing_lighting_hack = 0
   812→    then (
   813→      (* Exhaustive scan: find first segment where centermask=0 *)
   814→      let found = ref (-1) in
   815→      let s = ref 0 in
   816→      while !s < n_segments && !found = -1 do
   817→        let _children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions =
   818→          unpack_seg !s
   819→        in
   820→        let _facemask, _sidemask, centermask =
   821→          get_seg_masks
   822→            ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   823→            ~rad:0
   824→            ~seg_verts
   825→            ~side_types
   826→            ~normals
   827→            ~seg_vert_positions
   828→        in
   829→        if centermask = 0 then found := !s;
   830→        incr s
   831→      done;
   832→      !found)
   833→    else -1)
   834→  else if doing_lighting_hack = 0
   835→  then (
   836→    (* segnum=-1: no hint, go straight to exhaustive *)
   837→    let found = ref (-1) in
   838→    let s = ref 0 in
   839→    while !s < n_segments && !found = -1 do
   840→      let _children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = unpack_seg !s in
   841→      let _facemask, _sidemask, centermask =
   842→        get_seg_masks
   843→          ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   844→          ~rad:0
   845→          ~seg_verts
   846→          ~side_types
   847→          ~normals
   848→          ~seg_vert_positions
   849→      in
   850→      if centermask = 0 then found := !s;
   851→      incr s
   852→    done;
   853→    !found)
   854→  else -1
   855→;;
   856→
   857→(* find_point_seg_v2: same algorithm as find_point_seg but fetches segment
   858→   data on demand via Fetch_segment_data effects instead of a pre-packed array.
   859→   The 87-int segment data uses the same layout as FVI packed segments:
   860→     [0..5] children, [6..11] side_types, [12..19] seg_verts,
   861→     [20..55] normals (side*2, xyz each), [56..79] vert positions (8 verts, xyz each),
   862→     [80..85] wid, [86] first_object.
   863→   We only need the first 80 ints (same as the old find_point_seg format). *)
   864→let unpack_seg_from_fetch (seg_data : int array) =
   865→  let children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(i)) in
   866→  let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(6 + i)) in
   867→  let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(12 + i)) in
   868→  let normals =
   869→    Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
   870→      let off = 20 + (i * 3) in
   871→      seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   872→  in
   873→  let seg_vert_positions =
   874→    Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   875→      let off = 56 + (i * 3) in
   876→      seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   877→  in
   878→  children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions
   879→;;
   880→
   881→let find_point_seg_v2 ~point:(px, py, pz) ~segnum ~n_segments ~doing_lighting_hack =
   882→  let fetch s = Effect.perform (Fetch_segment_data s) |> unpack_seg_from_fetch in
   883→  let rec trace s iterations =
   884→    if s < 0 || s >= n_segments
   885→    then -1
   886→    else if iterations > 1024
   887→    then s
   888→    else (
   889→      let children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = fetch s in
   890→      let centermask, side_dists =
   891→        get_side_dists
   892→          ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   893→          ~seg_verts
   894→          ~side_types
   895→          ~normals
   896→          ~seg_vert_positions
   897→      in
   898→      if centermask = 0
   899→      then s
   900→      else (
   901→        let sd = Array.copy side_dists in
   902→        let result = ref (-1) in
   903→        let continue_ = ref true in
   904→        while !continue_ do
   905→          let biggest_side = ref (-1) in
   906→          let biggest_val = ref 0 in
   907→          let bit = ref 1 in
   908→          for sn = 0 to 5 do
   909→            if centermask land !bit <> 0 && children.(sn) > -1 && sd.(sn) < !biggest_val
   910→            then (
   911→              biggest_val := sd.(sn);
   912→              biggest_side := sn);
   913→            bit := !bit lsl 1
   914→          done;
   915→          if !biggest_side <> -1
   916→          then (
   917→            sd.(!biggest_side) <- 0;
   918→            let check = trace children.(!biggest_side) (iterations + 1) in
   919→            if check <> -1
   920→            then (
   921→              result := check;
   922→              continue_ := false))
   923→          else continue_ := false
   924→        done;
   925→        !result))
   926→  in
   927→  let exhaustive_scan () =
   928→    let found = ref (-1) in
   929→    let s = ref 0 in
   930→    while !s < n_segments && !found = -1 do
   931→      let _children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = fetch !s in
   932→      let _facemask, _sidemask, centermask =
   933→        get_seg_masks
   934→          ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   935→          ~rad:0
   936→          ~seg_verts
   937→          ~side_types
   938→          ~normals
   939→          ~seg_vert_positions
   940→      in
   941→      if centermask = 0 then found := !s;
   942→      incr s
   943→    done;
   944→    !found
   945→  in
   946→  if segnum >= 0 && segnum < n_segments
   947→  then (
   948→    let newseg = trace segnum 0 in
   949→    if newseg <> -1
   950→    then newseg
   951→    else if doing_lighting_hack = 0
   952→    then exhaustive_scan ()
   953→    else -1)
   954→  else if doing_lighting_hack = 0
   955→  then exhaustive_scan ()
   956→  else -1
   957→;;
   958→
   959→let max_vertices_per_segment = 8
   960→
   961→(* pick_random_point_in_seg: pick a random point inside a segment.
   962→   Computes the segment center, picks a random vertex, then moves
   963→   a random fraction toward that vertex.
   964→   C original: gameseg.cpp pick_random_point_in_seg *)
   965→let pick_random_point_in_seg ~segnum =
   966→  let seg_data = Effect.perform (Fetch_segment_data segnum) in
   967→  (* Extract 8 vertex positions from segment data *)
   968→  let verts =
   969→    Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   970→      let off = 56 + (i * 3) in
   971→      seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   972→  in
   973→  let center =
   974→    compute_segment_center
   975→      ~v0:verts.(0) ~v1:verts.(1) ~v2:verts.(2) ~v3:verts.(3)
   976→      ~v4:verts.(4) ~v5:verts.(5) ~v6:verts.(6) ~v7:verts.(7)
   977→  in
   978→  let vnum = (Ox_misc.p_rand () * max_vertices_per_segment) asr 15 in
   979→  let vec2 = Ox_math.vm_vec_sub ~a:verts.(vnum) ~b:center in
   980→  let vec2 = Ox_math.vm_vec_scale ~v:vec2 ~k:(Ox_misc.p_rand ()) in
   981→  Ox_math.vm_vec_add2 ~a:center ~b:vec2
   982→;;
   983→

```c
void wall_illusion_off(segment* seg, int side)
{

    int cside;
    
    csegp = &Segments[seg->children[side]];

    Walls[seg->sides[side].wall_num].flags |= WALL_ILLUSION_OFF;
    Walls[csegp->sides[cside].wall_num].flags |= WALL_ILLUSION_OFF;
}
```

D1 and D2 implementations are identical. These functions manipulate a linked list of attached objects (explosions attached to robots, etc.). They use `expl_info.attach_parent`, `expl_info.next_attach`, `expl_info.prev_attach`, and `attached_obj`.

This is a complex linked-list manipulation that mutates multiple objects' fields. To port this properly, I'd need effects to:
1. Fetch an object's attach info (flags, attached_obj, expl_info fields)
2. Write back modified attach info to multiple objects

This is a doubly-linked list removal. The function `obj_detach_one` removes a sub-object from the attached list:


- If next_attach != -1, set next's prev_attach = sub's prev_attach
- If prev_attach != -1, set prev's next_attach = sub's next_attach
- Else (first in list), set parent's attached_obj = sub's next_attach
- Clear sub's next_attach and prev_attach to -1
- Clear OF_ATTACHED flag from sub

`obj_detach_all` just loops while parent has attached objects. These functions are fairly self-contained and just manipulate the linked list structure, so they might be worth porting despite the complexity of reading and writing across multiple objects' fields.

    21→   Zeroes out PowerupsInMine/MaxPowerupsAllowed for old type,
    22→   sets id to POW_SHIELD_BOOST, updates vclip info.
    23→   D2 only (NETWORK version from multi.cpp). *)
    24→let bash_to_shield ~objnum =
    25→  (* Fetch [obj_id; shield_vclip_num; shield_frame_time] from C globals *)
    26→  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_bash_to_shield_data objnum) in
    27→  let old_type = data.(0) in

    41→(* clear_stuck_objects: Clear out all stuck objects. Called for a new ship.
    42→   Ported from main_d2/wall.cpp.
    43→   Fetches packed array: [num_stuck, then for each of 32 entries: wallnum, objnum, obj_type, obj_id]
    44→   Writes back: array of objnums that should get lifeleft = F1_0/8, followed by new num_stuck *)
    45→let clear_stuck_objects () =
    46→  (* Fetch stuck objects data from C:
    47→     data layout: [num_stuck_objects,
    48→       wallnum_0, objnum_0, obj_type_0, obj_id_0,
    49→       wallnum_1, objnum_1, obj_type_1, obj_id_1,
    50→       ...] *)
    51→  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_stuck_objects_data ()) in
    52→  let num_stuck = ref data.(0) in
    53→  let writeback = Array.create ~len:(max_stuck_objects + 2) (-1) in
    54→  let wb_count = ref 0 in
    55→  for i = 0 to max_stuck_objects - 1 do
    56→    let base = 1 + (i * 4) in
    57→    let wallnum = data.(base) in
    58→    if wallnum <> -1 then begin

    62→      if obj_type = obj_weapon && obj_id = flare_id then begin
    63→        writeback.(!wb_count) <- objnum;
    64→        incr wb_count
    65→      end;
    66→      (* wallnum will be cleared by C write-back handler *)
    67→      num_stuck := !num_stuck - 1
    68→    end
    69→  done;
    70→  (* Pack: [num_flares_to_set, objnum_0, objnum_1, ..., new_num_stuck] *)
    71→  let result = Array.create ~len:(!wb_count + 2) 0 in
    72→  result.(0) <- !wb_count;
    73→  for j = 0 to !wb_count - 1 do
    74→    result.(j + 1) <- writeback.(j)
    75→  done;
    76→  result.(!wb_count + 1) <- !num_stuck;
    77→  (* Assert: num_stuck should be 0 *)
    78→  if !num_stuck <> 0 then
    79→    Printf.eprintf "[OX] clear_stuck_objects: Num_stuck_objects=%d, expected 0\n" !num_stuck;

    87→(* special_reset_objects: Rebuild the free object list and update Highest_object_index.

    89→

    92→  | Write_special_reset_objects : int array -> unit Effect.t
    93→
    94→let max_objects = 350
    95→let obj_none = 255
    96→
    97→let special_reset_objects () =
    98→  (* Fetch: array of 350 ints = Objects[0..349].type *)
    99→  let obj_types = Effect.perform (Fetch_special_reset_objects_data ()) in
   100→  (* Assert Objects[0].type != OBJ_NONE *)
   101→  if obj_types.(0) = obj_none then
   102→    Printf.eprintf "[OX] special_reset_objects: Assert failed: Objects[0].type == OBJ_NONE\n";
   103→  (* Rebuild free list: iterate from MAX_OBJECTS-1 down to 0 *)
   104→  let num_objects = ref max_objects in
   105→  let highest = ref 0 in
   106→  let free_list = Array.create ~len:max_objects (-1) in
   107→  for i = max_objects - 1 downto 0 do
   108→    if obj_types.(i) = obj_none then begin
   109→      num_objects := !num_objects - 1;
   110→      free_list.(!num_objects) <- i
   111→    end else begin
   112→      if i > !highest then
   113→        highest := i
   114→    end
   115→  done;
   116→  (* Write back: [num_objects, highest_object_index, free_list[num_objects], free_list[num_objects+1], ...] *)
   117→  let n_free = max_objects - !num_objects in
   118→  let result = Array.create ~len:(2 + n_free) 0 in
   119→  result.(0) <- !num_objects;
   120→  result.(1) <- !highest;
   121→  for j = 0 to n_free - 1 do
   122→    result.(2 + j) <- free_list.(!num_objects + j)
   123→  done;
   124→  Effect.perform (Write_special_reset_objects result)

   127→(* update_player_stats: Update time_level/time_total, rolling over hours.
   128→   Ported from main_d1/gameseq.cpp and main_d2/gameseq.cpp (identical logic).

   130→   Write back: [time_level, hours_level, time_total, hours_total] *)
   131→

   133→  | Fetch_update_player_stats_data : unit -> int array Effect.t
   134→  | Write_update_player_stats : int array -> unit Effect.t
   135→
   136→let i2f x = x lsl 16

   138→let update_player_stats () =
   139→  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_update_player_stats_data ()) in
   140→  let frame_time = data.(0) in

   142→  let hours_level = ref data.(2) in
   143→  let time_total = ref data.(3) in
   144→  let hours_total = ref data.(4) in
   145→  time_level := !time_level + frame_time;
   146→  if !time_level > i2f 3600 then begin
   147→    time_level := !time_level - i2f 3600;
   148→    hours_level := !hours_level + 1
   149→  end;
   150→  time_total := !time_total + frame_time;
   151→  if !time_total > i2f 3600 then begin
   152→    time_total := !time_total - i2f 3600;
   153→    hours_total := !hours_total + 1
   154→  end;
   155→  Effect.perform (Write_update_player_stats
   156→    [| !time_level; !hours_level; !time_total; !hours_total |])
   157→;;

   169→let verify_console_object () =

   177→  if player_num <= -1 then
   178→    Printf.eprintf "[OX] verify_console_object: Assert failed: Player_num=%d <= -1\n" player_num;
   179→  (* Assert(Players[Player_num].objnum > -1) *)
   180→  if objnum <= -1 then
   181→    Printf.eprintf "[OX] verify_console_object: Assert failed: objnum=%d <= -1\n" objnum;
   182→  (* Set ConsoleObject = &Objects[Players[Player_num].objnum] *)

   184→  (* Assert(ConsoleObject->type == OBJ_PLAYER) *)
   185→  if obj_type <> obj_player then
   186→    Printf.eprintf "[OX] verify_console_object: Assert failed: type=%d != OBJ_PLAYER\n" obj_type;
   187→  (* Assert(ConsoleObject->id == Player_num) *)

   192→(* init_player_object: Set up Player_num & ConsoleObject.
   193→   If Player_num != 0, copy Players[Player_num] to Players[0] and set Player_num = 0.
   194→   Then set Players[0].objnum = 0, ConsoleObject = &Objects[0], and object fields.
   195→   Ported from main_d1/gameseq.cpp and main_d2/gameseq.cpp (identical logic). *)
   196→
   197→type _ Effect.t +=
   198→  | Fetch_init_player_object_data : unit -> int array Effect.t
   199→  | Write_init_player_object : int array -> unit Effect.t
   200→
   201→let max_players = 8
   202→let ct_flying = 4
   203→let mt_physics = 1
   204→
   205→let init_player_object () =
   206→  (* Fetch [Player_num] *)
   207→  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_init_player_object_data ()) in
   208→  let player_num = data.(0) in
   209→  (* Assert(Player_num >= 0 && Player_num < MAX_PLAYERS) *)
   210→  if player_num < 0 || player_num >= max_players then
   211→    Printf.eprintf "[OX] init_player_object: Assert failed: Player_num=%d out of range\n" player_num;
   212→  (* Write back [original_player_num, OBJ_PLAYER, CT_FLYING, MT_PHYSICS]
   213→     C handler: if player_num != 0, copies Players[player_num] to Players[0], sets Player_num = 0
   214→     Then sets Players[0].objnum = 0, ConsoleObject = &Objects[0], and object fields *)
   215→  Effect.perform (Write_init_player_object
   216→    [| player_num; obj_player; ct_flying; mt_physics |])

   219→let init_ammo_and_energy () =
   220→  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_init_ammo_energy_data ()) in
   221→  let energy = data.(0) in
   222→  let shields = data.(1) in
   223→  let secondary_ammo_0 = data.(2) in
   224→  let initial_energy = data.(3) in
   225→  let starting_shields = data.(4) in

   229→  let new_shields = if shields < starting_shields then starting_shields else shields in
   230→  let min_ammo = 2 + ndl - difficulty_level in

   232→  Effect.perform (Write_init_ammo_energy [| new_energy; new_shields; new_ammo |])

   235→(* free_object_slots: Scan the object list, freeing down to num_used objects.
   236→   Returns number of slots freed (D2) or 0 (D1, which returns void).
   237→   Ported from main_d1/object.cpp and main_d2/object.cpp.
   238→   Fetch data: [highest_object_index, is_d2, then per-object: type, flags, id, delete_objnum]
   239→   Write back: [return_value, n_marked, objnum_0, objnum_1, ...] *)
   240→
   241→type _ Effect.t +=
   242→  | Fetch_free_object_slots_data : unit -> int array Effect.t
   243→  | Write_free_object_slots : int array -> unit Effect.t
   244→
   245→let obj_wall = 0
   246→let obj_fireball = 1
   247→let obj_debris = 8
   248→let obj_flare = 10
   249→let of_should_be_dead = 2
   250→
   251→let free_object_slots ~num_used =
   252→  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_free_object_slots_data ()) in
   253→  let highest = data.(0) in
   254→  let is_d2 = data.(1) <> 0 in
   255→  let num_already_free = ref (max_objects - highest - 1) in
   256→  if max_objects - !num_already_free < num_used then
   257→    Effect.perform (Write_free_object_slots [| 0; 0 |])
   258→  else begin
   259→    let obj_list = Array.create ~len:max_objects 0 in
   260→    let olind = ref 0 in
   261→    let early_ret = ref false in
   262→    let ret_val = ref 0 in
   263→    let i = ref 0 in
   264→    while !i <= highest && not !early_ret do
   265→      let base = 2 + !i * 4 in
   266→      let obj_type = data.(base) in
   267→      let obj_flags = data.(base + 1) in
   268→      if obj_flags land of_should_be_dead <> 0 then begin
   269→        num_already_free := !num_already_free + 1;
   270→        if is_d2 && max_objects - !num_already_free < num_used then begin
   271→          ret_val := !num_already_free;
   272→          early_ret := true
   273→        end
   274→      end else begin
   275→        (match obj_type with
   276→        | t when t = obj_none ->
   277→          num_already_free := !num_already_free + 1;
   278→          if max_objects - !num_already_free < num_used then begin
   279→            ret_val := 0;
   280→            early_ret := true
   281→          end
   282→        | t when t = obj_wall || t = obj_flare ->
   283→          () (* Int3() - curious *)
   284→        | t when t = obj_fireball || t = obj_weapon || t = obj_debris ->
   285→          obj_list.(!olind) <- !i;
   286→          olind := !olind + 1
   287→        | _ -> () (* OBJ_ROBOT, OBJ_HOSTAGE, OBJ_PLAYER, etc. *)
   288→        )
   289→      end;
   290→      i := !i + 1
   291→    done;
   292→    if !early_ret then
   293→      Effect.perform (Write_free_object_slots [| !ret_val; 0 |])
   294→    else begin
   295→      let num_to_free = ref (max_objects - num_used - !num_already_free) in
   296→      let original_num_to_free = !num_to_free in
   297→      if !num_to_free > !olind then
   298→        num_to_free := !olind;
   299→      let mark_dead = Array.create ~len:max_objects 0 in
   300→      let n_marked = ref 0 in
   301→      (* Pass 1: free DEBRIS *)
   302→      for j = 0 to !num_to_free - 1 do
   303→        let base = 2 + obj_list.(j) * 4 in
   304→        if data.(base) = obj_debris then begin
   305→          num_to_free := !num_to_free - 1;
   306→          mark_dead.(!n_marked) <- obj_list.(j);
   307→          n_marked := !n_marked + 1
   308→        end
   309→      done;
   310→      if !num_to_free > 0 then begin
   311→        (* Pass 2: free FIREBALL with delete_objnum == -1 *)
   312→        for j = 0 to !num_to_free - 1 do
   313→          let base = 2 + obj_list.(j) * 4 in
   314→          let obj_type = data.(base) in
   315→          let delete_objnum = data.(base + 3) in
   316→          if obj_type = obj_fireball && delete_objnum = -1 then begin
   317→            num_to_free := !num_to_free - 1;
   318→            mark_dead.(!n_marked) <- obj_list.(j);
   319→            n_marked := !n_marked + 1
   320→          end
   321→        done;
   322→        if !num_to_free > 0 then begin
   323→          (* Pass 3: free WEAPON with id == FLARE_ID *)
   324→          for j = 0 to !num_to_free - 1 do
   325→            let base = 2 + obj_list.(j) * 4 in
   326→            let obj_type = data.(base) in
   327→            let obj_id = data.(base + 2) in
   328→            if obj_type = obj_weapon && obj_id = flare_id then begin
   329→              num_to_free := !num_to_free - 1;
   330→              mark_dead.(!n_marked) <- obj_list.(j);
   331→              n_marked := !n_marked + 1
   332→            end
   333→          done;
   334→          if !num_to_free > 0 then begin
   335→            (* Pass 4: free WEAPON with id != FLARE_ID *)
   336→            for j = 0 to !num_to_free - 1 do
   337→              let base = 2 + obj_list.(j) * 4 in
   338→              let obj_type = data.(base) in
   339→              let obj_id = data.(base + 2) in
   340→              if obj_type = obj_weapon && obj_id <> flare_id then begin
   341→                num_to_free := !num_to_free - 1;
   342→                mark_dead.(!n_marked) <- obj_list.(j);
   343→                n_marked := !n_marked + 1
   344→              end
   345→            done
   346→          end
   347→        end
   348→      end;
   349→      let rv = if is_d2 then original_num_to_free - !num_to_free else 0 in
   350→      let result = Array.create ~len:(2 + !n_marked) 0 in
   351→      result.(0) <- rv;
   352→      result.(1) <- !n_marked;
   353→      for j = 0 to !n_marked - 1 do
   354→        result.(2 + j) <- mark_dead.(j)
   355→      done;
   356→      Effect.perform (Write_free_object_slots result)
   357→    end
   358→  end
   359→;;
   360→
   361→(* filter_objects_from_level: Remove flag powerups from single-player levels.
   362→   Iterates all objects up to Highest_object_index and bashes any
   363→   POW_FLAG_RED or POW_FLAG_BLUE powerups to shield boosts.
   364→   D2 only. Ported from main_d2/gameseq.cpp. *)
   365→let filter_objects_from_level () =
   366→  (* Fetch [highest_object_index, type_0, id_0, type_1, id_1, ...] *)
   367→  let data = Effect.perform (Fetch_filter_objects_data ()) in
   368→  let highest_object_index = data.(0) in
   369→  for i = 0 to highest_object_index do
   370→    let obj_type = data.(1 + i * 2) in
   371→    let obj_id = data.(1 + i * 2 + 1) in
   372→    if obj_type = obj_powerup then
   373→      if obj_id = pow_flag_red || obj_id = pow_flag_blue then
   374→        bash_to_shield ~objnum:i
   375→  done
   376→;;
